methodology: Cleaned eval set v3. Removed 51 assertions that passed 100% across all 6 variants (3 models x with/without skill). Removed library-name-dropping assertions (Alamofire bug, Kingfisher/SDWebImage convergence). Removed tangential assertions (Combine .receive(on:), NSLock-vs-os_unfair_lock cross-reference trivia). Softened taxonomy assertions to test behavior not labels. Removed evals with 0 remaining assertions. Remaining assertions discriminate between with-skill and without-skill responses.

evals.name: queue-creation-medium
evals.expected_output: Identifies: (1) Scattered global() calls lead to thread explosion and no control, (2) Recommends consolidating to 3-4 named subsystem queues, (3) Should use target queue hierarchies to funnel work, (4) Notes barriers are silently ignored on global queues.

assertions.text: Recommends consolidating to a small number (3-4) of named subsystem queues
Suggests target queue hierarchies for funneling work
Notes that barriers are silently ignored on global queues

evals.name: queue-creation-complex
evals.expected_output: Identifies: (1) Target queue sync cycle deadlock - requestQueue targets rootQueue, so sync from rootQueue to requestQueue is nested sync on same underlying queue, (2) .background QoS inappropriate for user-visible work - may halt in Low Power Mode.

assertions.text: Mentions .background QoS may halt or be severely throttled in Low Power Mode

evals.name: deadlocks-simple
evals.expected_output: If called from main thread, main.sync deadlocks - main queue is serial, sync blocks waiting for the block to execute on the same queue that's already blocked.

assertions.text: Mentions libdispatch detects this case and crashes with EXC_BAD_INSTRUCTION
assertions.rationale: Skill-specific detail about libdispatch runtime detection

assertions.text: Suggests dispatchPrecondition for early detection of queue violations

evals.name: deadlocks-medium
evals.expected_output: Identifies nested sync on same serial queue - readFromCache holds cacheQueue, calls refreshCache which calls writeToCache which tries to sync on cacheQueue again.

assertions.text: Suggests dispatchPrecondition(.notOnQueue) to detect nested sync violations during development

evals.name: deadlocks-complex
evals.expected_output: Identifies: (1) ABBA lock ordering deadlock, (2) group.wait() on main thread risks deadlock, (3) results array data race, (4) recommends defer { group.leave() } pattern.

assertions.text: Recommends defer { group.leave() } pattern to guarantee leave on all code paths

evals.name: thread-safety-simple
evals.expected_output: This is correct. NSLock properly protects both read and write. Skill adds detail about priority donation and modern alternatives.

assertions.text: Mentions NSLock provides priority donation (via pthread_mutex_t)
Suggests OSAllocatedUnfairLock as alternative for iOS 16+

evals.name: thread-safety-complex
evals.expected_output: Identifies: (1) Barriers on global queue are silently ignored, (2) Must use custom concurrent queue, (3) global() calls may return different queue instances.

assertions.text: Identifies barriers on global queue are silently ignored (not just 'need a custom queue')
assertions.rationale: Must explicitly state barriers are silently ignored, not just recommend custom queue

assertions.text: Notes global() calls may return different queue instances, so there is no single queue identity

evals.name: thread-explosion-medium
evals.expected_output: Identifies: (1) Thread explosion from unbounded global().async, (2) DispatchSemaphore as mutex lacks priority donation, (3) Should use OperationQueue or concurrentPerform.

assertions.text: Identifies DispatchSemaphore used as mutex lacks priority donation, risking priority inversion
assertions.rationale: Skill explicitly warns against semaphore as mutex

evals.name: thread-explosion-complex
evals.expected_output: Identifies: (1) Thread explosion from 300+ async calls, (2) .background QoS may halt in Low Power Mode, (3) Scattered global() calls instead of consolidated queues, (4) Data(contentsOf:) is blocking, (5) No concurrency limit.

assertions.text: Notes .background QoS may halt or be severely throttled in Low Power Mode
Identifies scattered global() calls as an architectural problem that should be consolidated into named subsystem queues

evals.name: operation-queue-complex
evals.expected_output: Identifies: (1) super.start() should never be called, (2) State not thread-safe, (3) Missing isCancelled check, (4) Missing maxConcurrentOperationCount, (5) Should override cancel() to cancel in-flight URLSession task.

assertions.text: Overrides cancel() to cancel the in-flight URLSession task

evals.name: data-races-medium
evals.expected_output: Identifies: (1) data race on items/isLoading, (2) tableView.reloadData() on background, (3) dispatch to main queue, (4) dispatchPrecondition for verification.

assertions.text: Suggests dispatchPrecondition(.onQueue(.main)) for verifying main thread at API boundaries

evals.name: debugging-medium
evals.expected_output: Use dispatchPrecondition with .onQueue(.main) and .notOnQueue(.main). Removed in release builds.

assertions.text: Notes dispatchPrecondition is removed in release builds (zero production overhead)

evals.name: debugging-complex
evals.prompt:
I see this Thread Sanitizer output. Help me understand what's happening and how to fix it:

```
WARNING: ThreadSanitizer: data race (pid=12345)
  Write of size 8 at 0x7ffeefbff5a0 by thread T2:
    #0 MyApp.ImageCache.store(_:for:) ImageCache.swift:42
    #1 MyApp.NetworkManager.handleResponse(_:) NetworkManager.swift:87

  Previous read of size 8 at 0x7ffeefbff5a0 by main thread:
    #0 MyApp.ImageCache.image(for:) ImageCache.swift:28
    #1 MyApp.ProductCell.configure(with:) ProductCell.swift:15

  Location is heap block of size 64 at 0x7ffeefbff580
```
evals.expected_output: Explains: (1) Two threads accessed same memory, (2) ImageCache.store vs ImageCache.image call paths, (3) Fix with NSLock or serial queue, (4) dispatchPrecondition at API boundaries.

assertions.text: Suggests adding dispatchPrecondition at API boundaries for future protection
